document.title: CB Test Certificate and Test Report for Portable X-Ray system HnX-P1
document.documentType: IECEE CB Test Certificate and CB Test Report
document.revisionOrVersion: Ref. Certif. No. DK-163496-UL; Report No. ACTS-2024-SM-225; TRF No. IEC60601_1U

sourceSummary.oneLine: IECEE CB certificate and test report for H&abyz HnX-P1 Portable X-Ray system under IEC 60601-1 and related collateral and particular standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports overseas regulatory evidence of electrical safety, essential performance, radiation protection, usability, software lifecycle, and national difference compliance for the HnX-P1 portable X-ray system.
keyPoints: CB Test Certificate DK-163496-UL was issued by UL Solutions (Demko) on 2025-02-27.
Test Report ACTS-2024-SM-225 was issued by ACTS Co., Ltd. on 2025-02-24 under the CB Scheme.
The product tested was a Portable X-Ray system, model HnX-P1, rated internally powered 29.6 V d.c., 1470 mAh.
The certificate states that a sample was tested and found in conformity with IEC 60601-1, IEC 60601-1-3, IEC 60601-1-6, IEC 60601-2-28, and IEC 60601-2-54 standards.
Clause 11.7 biocompatibility and Clause 17 electromagnetic compatibility were not evaluated in the main IEC 60601-1 report.

evidence.claim: The product is intended for qualified or trained physicians or technicians to acquire X-ray images of patient anatomy and is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is a medical device intended for use by a qualified/trained physician or technician for the purpose of acquiring X-ray images ... This system is not intended for mammography.

evidence.claim: The product mode of operation is non-continuous.
evidence.evidenceText: Mode of operation: Non-continuous

evidence.claim: The main unit ingress protection classification is IPX0.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification according to the degree of protection against ingress of water: 1) IPX0: Main unit

evidence.claim: The equipment is not suitable for use in the presence of flammable anesthetics or oxygen.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment is not suitable for use in the presence of flammable anesthetises or oxygen.

evidence.claim: The expected service life stated in the risk management file is 7 years.
evidence.evidenceText: EXPECTED SERVICE LIFE stated in RISK MANAGEMENT FILE: 7 years

evidence.claim: Essential performance is identified as accuracy of X-ray radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: List of ESSENTIAL PERFORMANCE functions ... Accuracy of X-ray Radiation

evidence.claim: The rechargeable battery was certified according to IEC 62133-2 at TÜV SUD.
evidence.evidenceText: Rechargeable Battery This was certified in accordance with IEC 62133-2 at TÜ V SUD Refer to Ref. Certif. No. SG PSB-BT-05860

evidence.claim: The AC/DC adaptor was certified according to IEC 62368-1 at TÜV Rheinland.
evidence.evidenceText: AC/DC Adaptor This was certified in accordance with IEC 62368-1 at TÜ V Rheinland, Refer to Ref. Certif. No. JPTUV-125094-M2

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1 report did not evaluate biocompatibility or electromagnetic compatibility.
evidence.evidenceText: The following clauses were not evaluated in this report. - Clause 11.7, Biocompatibility - Clause 17, Electromagnetic compatibility

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-3 Annex A report covers radiation protection in diagnostic X-ray equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex A – IEC 60601-1-3 Test Report ... General Requirements for Radiation Protection in Diagnostic X-Ray Equipment

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 Annex B report covers usability and references IEC 62366-1:2015/AMD1:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex B – IEC 60601-1-6 Test Report ... Collateral standard: Usability; IEC 62366-1:2015, AMD1:2020 – Usability engineering process checklist

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-2-28 Annex C report covers safety of X-ray source assemblies and X-ray tube assemblies for medical diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex C – IEC 60601-2-28 Test Report ... Particular requirements for the safety of X-ray source assemblies and X-ray tube assemblies for medical diagnosis

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-2-54 Annex D report covers basic safety and essential performance of X-ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex D – IEC 60601-2-54 Test Report ... Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of X-ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy

evidence.claim: The product fulfilled EN IEC 60601-2-54:2024 in Annex D.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of EN IEC 60601-2-54:2024

evidence.claim: The product fulfilled EN 60601-1-3:2008/A1:2013/A11:2016/A2:2021 in Annex A.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of EN 60601-1-3:2008/A1:2013/A11:2016/A2:2021

evidence.claim: The product fulfilled EN 60601-1-6:2010/A1:2015/A2:2021 in Annex B.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of EN 60601-1-6:2010/A1:2015/A2:2021

reviewFlags.reason: The source is a combined certificate and test report; the document date field contains both the certificate date 2025-02-27 and test report issue date 2025-02-24.
Electromagnetic compatibility was not evaluated in the provided IEC 60601-1, IEC 60601-2-28, and IEC 60601-2-54 report sections; the text refers to a separate EMC test report.
Biocompatibility Clause 11.7 was not evaluated in the main IEC 60601-1 report.
The Korea national differences attachment is marked informative because the latest ND/GD TRF revision did not match the latest IEC Standard TRF used for evaluation.
